---
title: "Practice Problems: Bivariate Functions & Differentiation"
author: ""
output: 
  github_document:
    toc: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# This chunk sets up the environment.
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.align="center")

library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)

# Common plotting theme for consistency
common_theme <- theme_minimal() + 
                theme(axis.title = element_text(face="bold"),
                      plot.title = element_text(face="bold", hjust = 0.5))

```

1. Find the domain of the function $f(x, y) = \log(y - x^2)$. Describe the region geometrically and sketch it in the $x$-$y$ plane.

2. Calculate the first-order ($f_x, f_y$) and second-order ($f_{xx}, f_{yy}, f_{xy}$) partial derivatives for the following functions:
   (a) $f(x, y) = x^4 y^3 - 2x + 5y$
   (b) $f(x, y) = x e^{x^2 + y}$

3. Let
$$
f(x, y) = \frac{1}{24} xy^2 (3x^2 + y).
$$
Find the first-order partial derivatives ($f_x, f_y$) and the second-order partial derivatives ($f_{xx}, f_{yy}, f_{xy}$).

4. (More Challenging) Find the first-order ($f_x, f_y$) and second-order ($f_{xx}, f_{yy}, f_{xy}$) partial derivatives for the following functions:
   (a) $f(x, y) = \log(x^2 + y^2)$
   (b) $f(x, y) = \frac{x + y}{x - y}$
   (c) $f(x, y) = x e^{y/x}$
   (d) $f(x, y) = x \cos(\sqrt{xy})$

5. Show that the function $u(x, t) = \sin(x - ct)$ satisfies the wave equation $u_{tt} = c^2 u_{xx}$.
